dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8084 IH: Access to LARCs Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Hinson (for herself, Mrs. Miller-Meeks, Ms. Malliotakis, and Mr. Ciscomani) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct a study and submit to Congress a report on contraceptive access at community health centers in health care deserts.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Access to LARCs Act.

section.header: Study and report on access to range of contraceptive methods at community health centers

subsection.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (in this section referred to as the Secretary) shall conduct a study on the access of women in need to a range of contraceptive methods at community health centers located in health care deserts. Such study shall—

paragraph.text: include analysis related to reimbursement, inventory stocking, provider training, patient education, and other barriers to community health centers providing a range of contraceptive methods; and
indicate which community health centers are recipients of funding under title X of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300 et seq.).

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report describing the results of the study under subsection (a).

paragraph.text: The term community health center means a health center (as defined in section 330(a) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 254b(a))).

subparagraph.text: a drug or device (as such terms are defined in section 201 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 321)), or combination product, approved for use under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 321 et seq.) as a method of contraception, except when such drug, device, or combination product is used on- or off-label as an emergency contraceptive;
natural family planning or other fertility-based methods of family planning.

paragraph.text: The term health care desert means a State or political subdivision thereof with less than 1 community health center for every 1,000 women in need.
The term range of contraceptive methods means 2 or more contraceptive methods.
The term women in need means women eligible for benefits under a Federal health care program (as defined in section 1128B(f) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1320a–7b(f))).
